title: Outstanding western writers; The truth about the West
description: Buffalo Bill Cody was a promoter and a phoney. DeVoto, Dobie and Fisher were outstanding western writers. Truth about the West in books. Need to talk to so many people to get the real story of the area.

title: Potlatch Lumber Co. had to pay to use Mr. Kinman's land; Prune Joe, Bill Helmer's foreman
description: Mr. Kinman forces Potlatch Lumber Co. to pay for using his land for a log landing. Prune Joe, Bill Helmer's foreman.

title: Why the Potlatch mill was put in Potlatch instead of Moscow
description: Why the Potlatch mill was put in Potlatch instead of Moscow.

title: The Palouse River area before Potlatch; Riding hard for a doctor
description: The Palouse River country before Potlatch - it depended on Palouse, Wash. The old road to Palouse. Hard riding for a doctor.

title: Father homesteaded at Hooper, WA; Sold the homestead for better land near Palouse, WA
description: Father came here because the West was so "played up." He homesteaded at Hooper, Washington, selling the land so he could get better wheat land near Palouse.

title: Skills a boy needed before he could be called a man
description: A boy became a man when he could handle a team. Other skills he learned.

title: Youngsters did men's work
description: Youngsters did men's work.

title: Clearing land for wheat; Sales and failures of wheat after WWI
description: The slowness of clearing land, and the importance of land cleared. Father believed in wheat, but Glen distrusted it. Auction sales and failures of big wheat farms after WWI.

title: Father worked hard; Family had the necessities
description: Father a hard worker. Family had the necessities, which were few. Boys earned clothes by driving team.

title: Livestock on the farmland; Grinding flour
description: Small amount of livestock on the farm. Self-sufficiency. Grinding flour in Palouse.

title: Running around the country
description: Play - running around and seeing the country, what city people pay money for now. Trapping. Running to Troy from Harvard.

title: Becoming stranded while moving the household to Troy
description: Moving the household to Troy - getting stranded by using a sled on melting snow.

title: Fishing in the creeks; Potlatch Logging
description: Fishing in the creeks. Potlatch logging.

title: Miners on the Palouse River; the Mizpah Mines
description: Visiting with the miners on the Palouse River. Their independence, and the kind of people they were. History of the mining country. The Gold Bug and Mizpah Mines.

title: The Duff-Wagner feud
description: The Duff-Wagner feud.

title: Repairing the Palouse River road
description: Repairing the Palouse River road.

title: The Family farm near Harvard; Community social life
description: Site of the family farm near Harvard. Community social life - get togethers for parties and skating.

title: Having a chicken feed using stolen chicken
description: Having a chicken feed with the man they'd stolen the chickens from.

title: Meeting at the store for mail and talk at noon
description: Meeting at the store for mail and talk at noon. Discussing the world's problems.

title: Not much drinking the Harvard area
description: Drinking not very strong in Harvard area at dances - two stills, fewer than other places in the county.

title: Marriages in the area; Glen's shivaree
description: Courting - many marriages in the area. Glen's shivaree.

title: Quality of the Palousers
description: Quality of the Palousers.
